2604.21495.title: Generalizing Numerical Reasoning in Table Data through Operation Sketches and Self-Supervised Learning
2604.21495.paper_summary: Numerical reasoning over expert-domain tables often exhibits high in-domain accuracy but limited robustness to domain shift. Models trained with supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on specific datasets tend to rely on header-operation shortcuts rather than structural reasoning. We introduce TaNOS, a continual pre-training framework comprising three components: (i) header anonymization to reduce lexical memorization, (ii) operation sketches that provide minimal structural cues, and (iii) self-supervised pretraining that constructs correctness-guaranteed program-question pairs from given tables in a program-first manner. By decoupling domain semantics and numerical operation structure, TaNOS improves the transferability of numerical reasoning. Applied to an 8B instruction-tuned model, TaNOS achieves 80.13% execution accuracy on FinQA with only 10% train data, outperforming SFT baseline (73.97%) with full train data and proprietary models such as GPT-5, Gemini-2.5-Pro. Furthermore, in the domain-shift experiments, TaNOS displays nearly-negligible cross-domain gap (<2pp) when standard SFT shows over 10pp gap. These results suggest that structural guidance with operation sketches, header-agnostic representations, and correctness-guaranteed self-supervision can improve the robustness of numerical reasoning across diverse expert-domain tables.
2604.21495.paper_summary_zh: 數值推理在專家領域表格上通常顯示出高的領域內準確性，但對領域轉移的穩健性有限。使用特定數據集進行監督性微調（SFT）訓練的模型往往依賴於標題-操作快捷方式，而不是結構推理。我們提出了 TaNOS，一個持續預訓練框架，包含三個組件：（i）標題匿名化以減少詞彙記憶，（ii）提供最小結構提示的操作草圖，以及（iii）自我監督預訓練，從給定表格中以程序優先的方式構建正確性保證的程序-問題對。通過解耦領域語義和數值操作結構，TaNOS 改善了數值推理的可轉移性。應用於一個 8B 指令調整模型，TaNOS 在 FinQA 上達到 80.13% 的執行準確率，僅使用 10% 的訓練數據，超越了使用完整訓練數據的 SFT 基線（73.97%）和專有模型如 GPT-5、Gemini-2.5-Pro。此外，在領域轉移實驗中，當標準 SFT 顯示超過 10pp 的差距時，TaNOS 顯示出幾乎可以忽略的跨域差距（<2pp）。這些結果表明，使用操作草圖的結構指導、與標題無關的表示和正確性保證的自我監督可以提高數值推理在各種專家領域表格中的穩健性。
2604.21495.author: Hanjun Cho et.al.
2604.21495.authors: Hanjun Cho, Gahyun Yoo, Hanseong Kim, Jay-Yoon Lee
